steps.title: Compare inner fiber fineness across all three breeds
steps.content:
The table shows inner fiber diameter for each sheep type (smaller diameter = softer).

The Navajo-Churro's inner fiber ranges 10–35 microns, with a minimum of 10 microns.

The Mouflon's is 15 microns. The Merino's ranges 17–21 microns, with a minimum of 17 microns.

The Navajo-Churro's softest inner fiber is finer than both the Mouflon's and Merino's.

steps.title: Match this comparison to the answer choices
steps.content:
D directly states this finding: the Navajo-Churro's inner fiber, at its softest, is softer than either the Mouflon's or Merino's, which the table confirms (10 < 15 and 10 < 17).
  • A falsely describes Navajo-Churro's inner fiber as uniform when it ranges 10–35 microns
  • B contradicts the passage by calling Merino unsuitable for delicate garments when it's explicitly called ideal for them
  • C overstates overall softness when Navajo-Churro's outer fiber remains notably coarse
. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
